title: Spaghetti alla Carbonara Classic

seo.title: Classic Spaghetti Carbonara Recipe: Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Spaghetti Carbonara with our classic recipe! Learn how to make this creamy Italian pasta dish using traditional ingredients. Get the best tips!
seo.h1: Authentic Spaghetti Carbonara Recipe: A Classic Italian Dish
h2Headings: Pro Tips for Perfect Spaghetti Carbonara
seo.imageAlt: A steaming plate of authentic Spaghetti Carbonara with crispy guanciale, egg yolk, and grated Pecorino Romano cheese.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of this classic Roman Spaghetti Carbonara, made with traditional ingredients.

description: Spaghetti alla Carbonara is a quintessential Roman pasta dish, celebrated for its rich, creamy texture achieved without using actual cream. This classic recipe combines crispy guanciale, a velvety sauce of egg yolks and Pecorino Romano, and al dente spaghetti for an unforgettable culinary experience. It is a testament to the simplicity and flavor of traditional Italian cooking.

ingredients.notes: cured pork jowl, cut into small strips
finely grated, plus more for serving
for pasta water, use sparingly as guanciale and Pecorino are salty

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients and Sauce Mixture
instructions.text: Cut the guanciale into small strips or cubes. In a medium bowl, combine the three egg yolks and one whole egg. Add the finely grated Pecorino Romano cheese and a generous amount of freshly ground black pepper. Whisk vigorously until the mixture is smooth, creamy, and well combined. Set aside.

instructions.title: Cook the Guanciale
instructions.text: Place a large skillet over medium heat. Add the guanciale strips to the dry skillet. Cook slowly, stirring occasionally, until the guanciale is crispy and golden brown, and its fat has rendered out. This process typically takes about 8-10 minutes. Remove the crispy guanciale with a slotted spoon and set aside, leaving the rendered fat in the skillet.

instructions.title: Cook the Spaghetti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add a pinch of salt to the water (remembering guanciale and Pecorino are salty). Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until it is al dente. Before draining, reserve about 1 cup of the starchy pasta cooking water. Drain the spaghetti thoroughly.

instructions.title: Combine Pasta and Guanciale Fat
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the hot, drained spaghetti into the skillet containing the rendered guanciale fat. Toss the spaghetti vigorously with tongs, ensuring each strand is well coated with the flavorful fat. This also helps cool the pasta slightly before adding the egg mixture.

instructions.title: Create the Carbonara Sauce
instructions.text: Remove the skillet from the heat. Quickly pour the egg, cheese, and pepper mixture over the hot spaghetti. Add about 1/4 cup of the reserved pasta cooking water. Using tongs, toss the spaghetti continuously and vigorously. The residual heat from the pasta and the fat will gently cook the eggs, creating a silky, creamy sauce that coats the spaghetti without scrambling the eggs. This step is crucial for the perfect texture.

instructions.title: Adjust Consistency and Serve
instructions.text: Continue tossing, adding more pasta water a tablespoon at a time if the sauce appears too thick or dry, until it reaches your desired creamy consistency. Stir in most of the crispy guanciale, reserving some for garnish. Serve immediately in warm bowls, garnished with the remaining crispy guanciale, an extra sprinkle of grated Pecorino Romano, and a final generous grind of fresh black pepper. Enjoy your authentic Carbonara!

tips: For authentic flavor, always use guanciale. Its unique fat renders beautifully and provides a depth of flavor that pancetta or bacon cannot replicate.

The key to a perfectly creamy Carbonara without scrambling the eggs is to remove the skillet from direct heat before adding the egg mixture. The residual heat of the pasta and the pan, combined with constant tossing and a splash of starchy pasta water, creates the emulsified sauce.
Serve Carbonara immediately. This dish is at its best when fresh, as the sauce can thicken and lose its desired creaminess if left to sit for too long.
article: Spaghetti alla Carbonara Classic is a beloved Italian Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
